summary: Install-time exfiltration of host data, environment variables, and filesystem flag content to hardcoded remote IP
details: postinstall.js is wired into all three npm lifecycle hooks (preinstall/install/postinstall) and executes automatically on npm install. It collects host identifiers (hostname, cwd, uid), iterates process.env and captures any key matching FLAG|CTF|GLEAM|HTB|NPM|REGISTRY|VERDACCIO|INIT_CWD|PWD|CI|RUNNER|GITHUB (capturing GitHub Actions, npm registry, and CI runner secrets), and spawns child_process.execSync to grep /app, /workspace, /challenge, /home/runner/work/repo/repo and parent directories for CTF flag patterns. The combined payload is base64-encoded and PUT to http://154.57.164.76:32178/api/modules/ECT-839201 — a hardcoded bare-IP attacker endpoint on a non-standard port. A shipped publish-and-arm.sh script publishes the package to public npm and arms the tracking endpoint, structuring the campaign as a dependency-confusion probe against internal Verdaccio/private-registry resolutions.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
